title: Australasian Applied Statistics Conference 2026
text: We would like to cordially invite you to Queensland for the Australasian Applied Statistics Conference 2026 (AASC2026) to be held from the 30th November to the 4th December 2026 at the Mantra Mooloolaba Beach on the Sunshine Coast. AASC2026 is for anyone passionate about real-world data analysis, providing opportunities to exchange ideas in all areas of applied statistics, as well as encouraging networking and collaboration. Whether you are a student, researcher, industry representative, or simply have a keen interest in anything related to data science, AASC2026 will provide plenty of opportunities to learn, engage, share, and connect with fellow data science enthusiasts in a friendly and welcoming setting.

title: Australasian Applied Statistics Conference 2026
text:
Australian Data Science Network Conference 2026
Looking for another conference to attend just prior to AASC2026? The Australian Data Science Network (ADSN) 2026 conference will be held on Thursday the \(26^{th}\) and Friday the \(27^{th}\) November in Canberra the week before AASC2026. See the ADSN conference website for more information.

title: Registration
text:
Cancellation policy
Cancellations must be notified in writing to the conference organisers
Cancellations notified before 1st October 2026 will be given an 80% refund. After this date, but prior to 14th November the refund will be 50%. After 14th November 2026, there will be no refund. This policy applies to conference registrations and to the social program.

title: Registration
text:
While registering, accept AGUAi membership
Despite its name, the Australasian Genstat Users Association Inc (AGUAi) exists specifically to organise this biennial conference. As you register for the conference, please accept free membership. You will then be placed on the conference email list to stay informed about future AASC conferences and be eligible to vote on who gets to organise the next AASC. For historical reasons, the formal objectives of the association are:

To foster association between persons involved or interested in the application of statistical methods
To foster association between persons involved or interested in using Genstat
To organise meetings and events focused on statistical and statistical computing issues.

The Rules of Association are available here.

title: Workshops
text:
We are excited to announce the following pre-conference workshops will take place on Monday the \(30^{\text{th}}\) November.


Full-day ASReml-R Workshop

This one-day workshop will focus on modelling complex variance-covariance structures in biological experiments using ASReml-R. ASReml-R is a statistical library in R that fits linear mixed models using REML methodology. This workshop will cover key variance structures for random effects and residuals, including spatial, repeated measures, multivariate, and multi-environment models, with applications drawn from agricultural and medical research contexts.
The workshop is oriented toward practical analysis of real datasets. Participants are expected to have prior familiarity with linear mixed model methodology and R, and should bring their own laptop with R and ASReml-R pre-installed. A training ASReml-R license will be provided.

Morning (4 sessions)

Introduction & Linear Mixed Model Refresher
Brief ASReml-R orientation, mixed model theory, fixed vs random effects, and REML estimation.
Variance Structures in ASReml-R
The core topic considering diagonal, correlation, unstructured, and factor analytic structures.
Spatial Variance Structures
Modelling residual spatial correlation in field trials using AR1\(\times\)AR1 structures.
Repeated Measures & Random Regression
Modelling correlations over time, using simple or complex approaches.



Afternoon (3 sessions)

Bivariate & Multivariate Models
Random effects and residual correlation structures between responses.
Multi-Environment Trials & Factor Analytic Models
G\(\times\)E interaction, factor analytic variance structures, and extensions to other contexts.
Expanding to Complex Structures
Modelling multi-stratum designs with multiple layers of correlation between random effects and residuals.




Genstat Masterclass

This full-day masterclass will highlight some new additions and hidden features in Genstat throughout 4 sessions

New Features in Genstat 24

New graphics menus: irregular shade and tessellation plots, forest and pirate plots, ROC curves, adding shaded confidence regions to graphs
New statistical menus: bimodality and outlier tests, tabulating text, fitting smooth surfaces to irregular points, repeated measures with censoring
Menu updates: General ANOVA - transformations and multiple Y variates
New spreadsheet features

Advanced Data Handling in Genstat

Importing and exporting data in programs
Working with pointers and substitution
Useful functions when programming
Merging, appending, subsetting, stacking, and unstacking data in programs
Making the most of the spreadsheet facilities

Building Your Own Graphs by Programming

Better keys with DKEY
Labelling points and controlling label positioning
Non-standard plotting symbols and icons
Adding text, arrows, error bars, lines and shaded regions to graphs
Overlaying graphs

Extra Features When Fitting Mixed Models

Fitting \(p\)-splines and other no-standard models
Plotting spline fits
Exploring fixed and random effects
Outlier detection
Power analysis for a fixed term or contrast





From fishing trips to stock indicators: An introduction to fisheries catch rate standardisation in R

This interactive half-day workshop will introduce you to the standardisation of fisheries catch rates (CPUE) using R. You’ll work with a realistic simulated catch-and-effort dataset and step through the key stages and decisions of a CPUE standardisation workflow, including:

Filtering data
Considering model structure and covariates
Fitting simple CPUE linear models, and
Generating annual predictions

You’ll gain an applied understanding of why catch rates often need to be standardised, how to fit and interpret simple CPUE standardisation models in R, how to generate and visualise annual standardised indices, and how those indices are used within a stock assessment context. We’ll explore some conceptual conundrums, like targeting behaviour and fishing efficiency. The content will focus on practical modelling decisions and reproducible analysis.
The workshop will be accessible to participants without a fisheries background. A basic understanding of R would be helpful.

title: Social activities
text:
We are excited to announce the following social activities will be available during the conference on the Wednesday afternoon:




#


Name


Description


Time and Duration


Location


Price






1


Walking along the oceanfront


Enjoy a guided group walk, setting off from the conference venue, and exploring the oceanfront along Mooloolaba and Maroochydore! The whole group will stay together from 1:30pm-3:30pm, then there will be an option to continue walking or stop for refreshments. Group schedule for walking: 1:30pm – 2:00pm: Explore the rocky beach front across from the Mantra Mooloolaba (conference venue). 2:00pm – 3:30pm: Walk north along ocean front heading to Maroochydore Beach (approx. 45 min one-way) and back, exploring beaches, cafés and shops. 3:30pm – onwards: Either walk south to The Wharf (approx. 15 min) and have refreshments, or walk to the mouth of Mooloolaba River (approx. 35 minutes one-way) and then to The Wharf to have refreshments.


1:30pm–3:30pm


Mantra Mooloolaba


No cost for the walking. Self-funding required for cafés, shops, and refreshments.




2


Aquaduck


Explore Mooloolaba by land and sea on the Aquaduck Sunshine Coast. Departing directly in front of SEA LIFE Sunshine Coast, Aquaduck combines the best sights of Mooloolaba into one fun and exciting tour. More information: https://www.aquaduck.com.au/sunshine-coast/


Departure time: 2pm (check in 20 mins before departure, which will be 1:40pm). Duration: 1 hour.


Departure location: Near the Mooloolaba Aquarium, but the street side.


Group bookings available: with a minimum of 15, max of 29, and there will be a discount on the $56/head but depends on group size. Bar not available.




3


Mooloolaba Canal Cruise at Sunset


Leave the land behind for a relaxing change of pace cruising through the calm waters of the Mooloolah River on Mooloolaba’s most popular canal cruise. Board your cruise from the Mooloolaba Wharf for a tranquil journey through the heart of Mooloolaba. Step onboard our 1-hour cruise and immerse yourself in our Skipper’s live commentary, ensuring you not only see the sights but also learn about the region’s rich history and significance. More information: https://www.mooloolabacanalcruise.com/


Departure time: sunset (arrive 15 mins before departure, which will be ~6:10pm). Duration: 1 hour.


The Wharf Mooloolaba (near the aquarium). The exact address is 123 Parkyn Parade Mooloolaba, QLD, 4557. If you head through the Wharf complex towards the water, you will see our beautiful vessel the M.V.Mudjimba (located behind Rice Boi restaurant).


$35 per person for a group booking of min 15 and max 35. Bar available.




4


Ginger Factory


One of the Sunshine Coast’s most popular attractions, The Ginger Factory has been delighting families for more than 40 years. Home of Buderim Ginger, the World’s Finest Ginger™, visitors can explore the beautiful nine-hectare property and factory on our tours and rides. It is the world’s largest operating ginger factory and produces a large range of delicious and locally made ginger drinks, sweets, spreads and foods. The iconic Ginger Factory also boasts boutique shopping, a café and ice creamery, lush sub-tropical gardens and exciting rides. Tours (group will do together): Tastes of Ginger tour (40 mins), Live Bee Show (40 mins), Ginger Train Ride (15 mins). More information: https://www.gingerfactory.com.au/


Bus departure time: 1pm. Arrive at Ginger Factory: 1:30pm. Leave Ginger Factory via bus at 4:15pm. Return to Mantra Mooloolaba 5:15pm.


Bus departure location: Mantra Mooloolaba. Location of Ginger Factory: 50 Pioneer Road, Yandina, Sunshine Coast, QLD 4561. Bus return location: Mantra Mooloolaba.


$72.50 (Includes: Bus fare $32.50, Taste of Ginger tour $15, Live Bee Show $15, Ginger Train Ride $10).




5


Montville


Free time in Montville to explore the many shops and art galleries along the main strip. Suggested group afternoon tea at Poets Café at 3:30pm (optional). More information: Montville Shopping, Montville Dining


Bus departure time: 1pm. Arrive at Montville 2:15pm. Afternoon tea at Poets Café 3:30pm. Leave Montville via bus at 5pm. Return to Mantra Mooloolaba 5:45pm.


Bus departure location: Mantra Mooloolaba. Montville is centrally placed on Queensland’s Sunshine Coast, about ~30km west of Mantra Mooloolaba. Bus return location: Mantra Mooloolaba.


$32.50 (bus fare). Self-funding required for cafés, shops, etc.
